manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: bark, bat, rail, bolt, cock, fly, strain, jerk, stem.
Your opponent's team words are: pro, rally, pound, peep, fit, stunt, fleet, beetle.
Distractor words are: slip, cuff, post, plot, pit, jog, rubber.
The kill words are: ham.

message.content:
CLUE: wood
TARGETS: bark, stem, bat
message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue word related to some of our team's words: bark, bat, rail, bolt, cock, fly, strain, jerk, stem. Must be single word not any of the listed words or containing them. Also can't contain parts of them? "cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them." So avoid substrings like "bat", "rail", etc. Provide list of target words we think clue relates to.

We want maximize number but avoid causing opponent guess their words. Also avoid kill word ham.

Need to think of a clue that connects multiple of our words.

Potential theme: "animal" connects bark (dog), bat (animal), cock (rooster), fly (insect), maybe "strain" not animal. "bolt" not animal.

Maybe "bird" connects bat? No bat is mammal. "farm" connects cock, bat? Probably not.

Maybe "machine" connects bolt, rail, jerk? Not.

"Stress" relates to strain, jerk, maybe "bolt" (bolt of lightning stress?). Not.

"Wood" connects bark, stem, maybe rail (wood rail). bolt? Not.

"Power" connects bolt (lightning bolt), jerk (quick movement), strain (force). Could also connect "fly" (fly power? no). "cock" (cock of a gun, power). Might be ambiguous.

Better: "horse" - connects "cock" (cockhorse?), not.

Maybe "speed": bolt (as in Usain Bolt), jerk (quick movement), fly (to move fast), rail (railgun? high speed), bat (baseball bat swing speed). Many.

But need to avoid opponent terms like "fleet" (speed). They could guess opponent's word if clue too generic. But that's okay as long as they guess correct word first? If they guess opponent's word, turn ends and opposing team gets to make a turn. So avoid clue that may lead them to opponent words. Opponent words: pro, rally, pound, peep, fit, stunt, fleet, beetle. So "fleet" is speed. So a speed clue may cause them to guess "fleet". That would be bad.

Maybe "forest": relates to bark, stem, maybe rail (railway through forest), bat (forest bat), cock? Not. "fly" maybe insects in forest. It's okay.

But we need single-word clue, not phrase. "Forest" could be good; relates to bark, stem, bat, maybe also "fly" (forest fly). Might be ambiguous with opponent's "beetle" (in forest). But that's okay; they might guess beetle but it's opponent; they'd end turn and we lose momentum.

Better to choose limited subset.

Maybe choose clue "tree" which connects bark, stem, maybe "rail" (rail made of wood), "bat" (bat roosts in trees), "cock" (rooster not tree). "Fly" (treefly?). Might be okay.

What about "construction": bolt, rail, stem (structural), jerk (force). Could be.

But we need to list which target words we want them to guess. Possibly 3 or 4.

We can give clue "wood" connecting bark, stem, maybe rail (wooden rail), bat (wooden bat), maybe "cock" (wooden cock? not). That's many.

Could be risk that "bat" also could be baseball bat (wood). Good.

Thus clue "wood" -> targets: bark, stem, bat, rail? But rail maybe not obvious wood? Could be.

But we might stick to three: bark, stem, bat. All clearly wood-related. That seems safe.

Clue can't be part of any words: "wood" is fine.

So answer:

CLUE: wood
TARGETS: bark, stem, bat

Make sure formatting exactly.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'wood'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

rally, plot, pro, stem, strain, fit, beetle, rubber, stunt, fleet, jerk, pit, bat, jog, cuff, cock, slip, bark, post, peep, bolt, ham, pound, rail, fly

message.reasoning: We need to output "GUESS: " then up to 3 words from the list that best relate to 'wood'. Words that are closely associated: bark (tree), stem (part of plant), post (wooden post). Could also be "bat" (made of wood), "rail" (wooden rail), "bolt" maybe wood bolt. Choose three: bark, stem, post. Need comma-separated list after GUESS: with no other text. So "GUESS: bark, stem, post". Ensure correct format
